proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with flt3_itd=0; we expect flt3_itd+ to have lower response (poor-prognosis marker).
Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0 (favorable-prognosis marker).
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0 (adverse-prognosis marker).
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with secondary_aml=0.
Older age (higher age_years) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with idh1_mutation=0.
Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with idh2_mutation=0.
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0.

analyses.result_summary: Secondary AML ORR 0.169 (n=12301) vs de novo 0.169 (n=37699). Diff 0.000, NS.
Age has no detectable main effect on ORR (beta ~0.0004 per year).
Higher ECOG strongly reduces ORR (beta -0.384 per ECOG point; ORR drops from 0.213 at ECOG 0 to 0.112 at ECOG 2).
IDH1+ ORR 0.221 (n=3624) vs IDH1- 0.165 (n=46376). Diff +0.056. Strongly higher response among IDH1-mutant patients.
Unfit ORR 0.169 (n=20056) vs fit 0.169 (n=29944). No marginal difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among flt3_itd+ patients, those receiving treatment_midostaurin=1 have a higher objective_response rate than flt3_itd+ patients not receiving midostaurin (targeted-therapy benefit).
Among flt3_itd+ patients, those receiving treatment_gilteritinib=1 have a higher objective_response rate than flt3_itd+ patients not receiving gilteritinib.
Among idh1_mutation+ patients, those receiving treatment_ivosidenib=1 have a higher objective_response rate than idh1+ patients not receiving ivosidenib.
Among idh2_mutation+ patients, those receiving treatment_enasidenib=1 have a higher objective_response rate than idh2+ patients not receiving enasidenib.
Among flt3_tkd+ patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 raises objective_response.

analyses.code: subset flt3_itd==1; chi-square on midostaurin x ORR
analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD+ on midostaurin ORR 0.161 vs FLT3-ITD+ not on midostaurin 0.174. Trend in opposite direction; NS.

analyses.code: subset flt3_itd==1; chi-square on gilteritinib x ORR
analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD+ on gilteritinib ORR 0.169 vs FLT3-ITD+ not on gilteritinib 0.172. No benefit; NS.

analyses.code: subset idh1_mutation==1; chi-square on ivosidenib x ORR
analyses.result_summary: IDH1+ on ivosidenib ORR 0.174 vs IDH1+ not on ivosidenib 0.225. Numerically WORSE on the targeted drug, borderline.

analyses.code: subset idh2_mutation==1; chi-square on enasidenib x ORR
analyses.result_summary: IDH2+ on enasidenib ORR 0.144 vs IDH2+ not on enasidenib 0.167. Numerically lower on the targeted drug; NS.

analyses.code: subset flt3_tkd==1; chi-square on gilteritinib x ORR
analyses.result_summary: FLT3-TKD+ on gilteritinib ORR 0.141 vs not 0.166. No benefit; NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 increases objective_response relative to unfit patients not receiving venetoclax/azacitidine.
Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 reduces objective_response (intensive chemo is harmful in unfit patients).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a positive main effect on objective_response after adjusting for age and ECOG.
treatment_7plus3 has a positive main effect on objective_response after adjusting for age and ECOG.

analyses.code: subset unfit_for_intensive==1; chi-square on venetoclax_azacitidine x ORR
analyses.result_summary: Unfit on venetoclax/aza ORR 0.179 (n=8013) vs unfit not on it 0.163 (n=12043). Diff +0.016, significant.

analyses.code: subset unfit_for_intensive==1; chi-square on 7plus3 x ORR
analyses.result_summary: Unfit on 7+3 ORR 0.169 (n=9106) vs unfit not on 7+3 0.170 (n=10950). No detectable harm or benefit.
venetoclax/aza beta +0.056 (OR ~1.06) after adjusting for age and ECOG; small but significant positive main effect.
7+3 beta +0.018, NS after adjusting for age and ECOG.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul (peripheral blast burden) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response (tumor burden marker).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: weight_loss_pct_6mo beta -0.040 per percent; strong negative association.
blast_pct_marrow beta -0.0057 per percent; strong negative association.
albumin beta +0.115 per g/dL; higher albumin associated with higher response.
CRP beta -0.0070 per mg/L; higher CRP lowers response.
WBC beta -0.0022 per K/uL; higher WBC lowers response.
LDH not associated with ORR.
NLR not associated with ORR.

proposed_hypotheses.text: race_ethnicity is associated with objective_response: white patients have a different ORR than non-white patients.
insurance_type is associated with objective_response.
rural_residence=1 patients have a different ORR than urban patients.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response.
atrial_fibrillation=1 is associated with a lower objective_response.
sex_female=1 is associated with a different objective_response.

analyses.code: chi-square on white vs non-white x ORR
analyses.result_summary: White ORR 0.173 (n=32498) vs non-white 0.162 (n=17502). White-vs-non-white diff +0.011, p=2.1e-3.
Across medicaid 0.169, medicare 0.172, private 0.167, uninsured 0.166. Differences small and not significant.
Rural ORR 0.169 vs urban 0.169. No difference.
appetite_loss_grade beta -0.023; small negative association, marginally significant.
fatigue_grade no association with ORR.
Female ORR 0.171 vs male 0.168. NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, albumin, CRP, weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, and wbc_k_per_ul, white race remains associated with higher objective_response than non-white race.

In a multivariable logistic model that jointly adjusts for demographic, fitness, biomarker, lab, and treatment variables, idh1_mutation retains an independent positive association with objective_response.
In a multivariable logistic model, ecog_ps retains an independent strong negative association with objective_response, dwarfing all biomarker effects.

In a multivariable logistic model, none of treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, or treatment_7plus3 retains a significant main effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(ORR ~ white + age + sex + ECOG + albumin + crp + weight_loss + blast_pct + wbc)
analyses.result_summary: White-race indicator beta +0.0755 (p=0.003) after adjustment; disparity persists.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit including all biomarkers, labs, and treatments
analyses.result_summary: idh1_mutation beta +0.360 (OR ~1.43) in full model; remains the dominant biomarker effect.
ecog_ps beta -0.383 (per point); by far the largest effect in the model.

Adjusted main effects: midostaurin -0.005 (p=0.89), gilteritinib -0.052 (p=0.13), ivosidenib +0.018 (p=0.69), enasidenib -0.056 (p=0.22), 7+3 +0.017 (p=0.48). Only venetoclax/aza retained borderline positive main effect (+0.059, p=0.016).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between idh1_mutation and treatment_ivosidenib on objective_response (added benefit of ivosidenib in IDH1-mutant patients).
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between idh2_mutation and treatment_enasidenib on objective_response.
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between flt3_itd and treatment_midostaurin on objective_response.
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between flt3_itd and treatment_gilteritinib on objective_response.

After adjusting for many covariates, idh1_mutation continues to be associated with higher objective_response independent of treatment_ivosidenib (i.e., it is a prognostic, not predictive, marker in this cohort).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -0.364 (p=0.027); IDH1-positive patients fare WORSE on ivosidenib than IDH1-positive patients off ivosidenib. Direction opposite to clinical expectation.
Interaction -0.124, NS. No predictive benefit of enasidenib in IDH2-mutant patients.
Interaction -0.105, NS. No predictive benefit of midostaurin in FLT3-ITD-mutant patients.
Interaction +0.034, NS. No predictive benefit of gilteritinib in FLT3-ITD patients.
IDH1 main effect beta +0.361 remains highly significant; ivosidenib effect ~0 (p=0.69). IDH1 acts as a prognostic, not a treatment-predictive, marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the candidate SNPs (snp_rs* columns), at least one shows an association with objective_response after a univariable scan; in particular snp_rs1050828 carrier status is associated with higher objective_response.

There is a positive multiplicative interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and unfit_for_intensive on objective_response (i.e., the venetoclax/aza benefit is concentrated in unfit patients).
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and age>=75 on objective_response.
There is a negative multiplicative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and age>=75 on objective_response (intensive chemo harms older patients).

analyses.code: univariable chi-square scan over all snp_rs* columns; report top hits
analyses.result_summary: Best signal snp_rs1050828: ORR 0.185 (n=4025) vs non-carriers 0.168 (diff +0.017, p=0.006). After Bonferroni across ~25 SNPs (alpha 0.002) it is not robust. No SNP survives multiple-testing correction.
Interaction +0.104 (p=0.036). venetoclax/aza specifically helps unfit patients (16% absolute lift in unfit subgroup vs no lift in fit). Matches the clinical indication.
Interaction -0.056, NS. The venetoclax/aza benefit is not specific to >=75 yr.
Interaction +0.041, NS. No detectable harm of intensive chemo in older patients in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and npm1_mutation on objective_response (NPM1-mutant disease is sensitive to venetoclax-based therapy).
There is a negative multiplicative interaction between treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and tp53_mutation on objective_response (TP53-mutant disease resists venetoclax).
There is a negative multiplicative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and complex_karyotype on objective_response (intensive chemo is less effective for adverse cytogenetics).
There is a negative multiplicative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and ecog_ps on objective_response (intensive chemo less tolerated at higher ECOG).
There is a negative multiplicative interaction between treatment_7plus3 and secondary_aml on objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(ORR ~ ven_aza + npm1 + ven_aza*npm1) + stratified rates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction +0.261 (p=6.6e-7). Stratified: NPM1+/ven_aza ORR 0.193 (n=6025) vs NPM1+/no-ven 0.160 (n=8989, diff +0.034, p=9.8e-8). Strong predictive effect.
Interaction -0.087, NS. No detectable TP53 resistance to venetoclax/aza in this cohort.
Interaction -0.213 (p=1.6e-3). Among CK+ patients, 7+3 ORR 0.155 vs no-7+3 0.179 (diff -0.023, p=0.008). Intensive chemo specifically underperforms in adverse cytogenetics.
Interaction -0.018, NS. No detectable 7+3-by-ECOG interaction (ECOG hurts all patients similarly).
Interaction -0.068, NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single multivariable logistic model that jointly includes age, sex, ECOG, fitness flag, all major biomarkers, key labs, all treatments, and four key interaction terms (ven_aza*npm1, ven_aza*unfit, 7plus3*complex_karyotype, idh1*ivosidenib), the four-pronged interaction structure replicates the patterns identified earlier: ven_aza*npm1 positive, ven_aza*unfit positive, 7plus3*complex_karyotype negative, and idh1*ivosidenib negative.

After adjusting for the standard clinical/biomarker covariates, the predictive structure of treatment effects in this AML cohort departs from accepted clinical pharmacology: targeted therapies for IDH1, IDH2, and FLT3 do not produce any detectable benefit in their nominally targeted subgroups.

Across the cohort, the strongest determinant of objective_response is performance status (ecog_ps) plus systemic-illness markers (weight_loss_pct_6mo, blast_pct_marrow, albumin_g_dl, crp_mg_l, wbc_k_per_ul), not classical AML risk genetics (TP53, complex karyotype, secondary AML, FLT3-ITD, age).

analyses.code: Logit(ORR ~ all main effects + ven_aza*npm1 + ven_aza*unfit + 7plus3*CK + idh1*ivo)
analyses.result_summary: Final model: ven_aza*npm1 +0.267 (p=4.5e-7), 7plus3*CK -0.219 (p=1.4e-3), idh1*ivo -0.356 (p=0.032), ven_aza*unfit +0.104 (p=0.038). All four interactions significant in expected directions for clinical fitness and the standard NPM1-venetoclax effect, but with the unexpected IDH1-ivosidenib reversal preserved.

analyses.code: Same final model, inspect treatment-by-biomarker interactions and main effects
analyses.result_summary: Targeted-therapy main effects all NS (midostaurin -0.003 p=0.92; gilteritinib -0.052 p=0.13; ivosidenib +0.049 p=0.29; enasidenib -0.057 p=0.21). Targeted interactions for FLT3/midostaurin, FLT3/gilteritinib, IDH2/enasidenib all NS; IDH1/ivosidenib interaction is significantly negative. Net effect: no benefit, and possibly slight harm, of targeted therapy in the targeted subgroup.

analyses.code: Multivariable Logit; rank coefficients by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top predictors: ecog_ps p=6e-97 (beta -0.38), weight_loss_pct_6mo p=1e-34 (beta -0.04), blast_pct_marrow p=5e-20 (beta -0.006), idh1_mutation p=2e-18 (beta +0.39), albumin p=1e-6 (beta +0.12), crp p=1e-6 (beta -0.007), wbc p=1e-5 (beta -0.002). Classical adverse-genetics markers (TP53, CK, secondary AML, FLT3-ITD, age) are not significant. IDH1 acts as a prognostic, not a predictive, marker.
